article_title: Boston Dynamics Publishes Atlas Training Playbook: GPU-Parallelized Reinforcement Learning Enables 100-Pound Loads in Weeks
summary: All content checks pass; allowlist warning is a configuration issue (reputable primary sources), not a content problem

content_preview.title: Boston Dynamics Publishes Atlas Training Playbook: GPU-Parallelized Reinforcement Learning Enables 100-Pound Loads in Weeks
content_preview.summary: Boston Dynamics engineers detail the five-step reinforcement learning pipeline that taught Atlas to lift over 100 pounds using whole-body control — from animation to factory floor in weeks.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Boston Dynamics has published a detailed technical account of how its Atlas humanoid robot learned to handle heavy industrial loads — lifting a mini-fridge weighing over 100 pounds using whole-body coordination rather than arm strength alone. The company's Director of Robot Behavior for Atlas, Alberto Rodriguez, along with research engineers Shane Rozen-Levy and Vinay Kamidi, describe a five-step reinforcement learning pipeline that compressed what would traditionally take months of...

recommendations: Consider adding bostondynamics.com and techtimes.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — both are reputable primary/established sources

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, appropriately detailed technical news article. The five-step RL pipeline is explained clearly without over-simplifying. The 'Production Context and What We Don't Know' and 'Analysis' sections show editorial maturity by explicitly flagging open questions and contextualizing the commercial significance.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources verified from disk snapshots (source-0.html.gz through source-3.html.gz, all status 200). Source-0 (Boston Dynamics engineering blog, bostondynamics.com): primary source, confirms all five pipeline steps, all direct quotes verbatim, robot specs (90 kg weight, two actuator types, infinite rotation, field-replaceable modules), and 'train in a day' aspiration. Source-1 (Interesting Engineering, interestingengineering.com): confirms 50-70 lb training range, 100+ lb real-world test, corroborates BD blog quotes. Source-2 (Tech Times, techtimes.com): confirms 56 degrees of freedom and 2.3-meter reach, Benjamin Stephens quote verbatim ('Put your whole body into it, was kind of the idea'), ~4 units/month production figure, Hyundai 30,000/year by 2028 target, Savannah Georgia RMAC, parts sequencing 2028 and component assembly 2030. Source-3 (Robotics and Automation News, roboticsandautomationnews.com): corroborates demo description and hardware design. All attributed quotes confirmed verbatim. The allowlist warning for bostondynamics.com and techtimes.com is a configuration gap, not a credibility issue — both are reputable sources (one is the company's own engineering blog).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims verified against sources: Alberto Rodriguez (Director of Robot Behavior), Shane Rozen-Levy and Vinay Kamidi (Research Engineers) confirmed in source-0 byline. Policy trained on 50-70 lb loads, successfully handled 100+ lb in testing confirmed in sources 1 and 2. 56 degrees of freedom and 2.3-meter reach confirmed in source-2. Benjamin Stephens title (Atlas Controls Associate Director) and quote confirmed in source-2. Four Atlas units/month production rate attributed to 'former employees' in source-2, correctly framed as approximate in the article. No hallucinations or fabrications detected across any claim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The Analysis section accurately contextualizes the significance of the training methodology publication without editorializing. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a strong example of responsible tech journalism.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. The April 2026 article (03-hyundai-targets-30000-atlas-robots-per-year) covers production ramp and commercial context. This article covers the training methodology — a distinct engineering story triggered by the BD blog publication. No overlap concern.
recommendations: Add bostondynamics.com and techtimes.com to config/source_allowlist.txt to avoid allowlist warnings on future Boston Dynamics coverage
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission. All sources verified, all quotes confirmed verbatim, all specifics traced to cited sources. APPROVE.
